NAMES.name: poloidal_magnetic_field
NAMES.short: Poloidal component of the magnetic field, $B_\theta$.
NAMES.long: The poloidal component of the magnetic field $\vec{B}$ — i.e. the projection onto the poloidal plane. In tokamak geometry this is the field arising primarily from the plasma current.
NAMES.sign: Positive in the direction of increasing poloidal angle in a right-handed cylindrical frame.

NAMES.name: toroidal_magnetic_field
NAMES.short: Toroidal component of the magnetic field, $B_\phi$.
NAMES.long: The toroidal component of the magnetic field $\vec{B}$ — the dominant component in a tokamak, set by the toroidal field coils.
NAMES.sign: Positive in the direction defined by COCOS convention.

NAMES.name: magnetic_field
NAMES.long: The magnetic field $\vec{B}$ throughout the plasma volume. Decomposes into poloidal and toroidal components in tokamak geometry.

NAMES.name: safety_factor_at_magnetic_axis
NAMES.short: Safety factor $q_0$ at the magnetic axis.
NAMES.long: The safety factor evaluated at the magnetic axis, $q_0 = q(\psi=0)$. Sensitive indicator of central current density and onset of sawtooth instability when crossing unity.
NAMES.sign: Positive for conventional tokamak operation; sign depends on the COCOS convention used.

NAMES.name: safety_factor
NAMES.short: Safety factor $q(\psi)$ — the ratio of toroidal to poloidal field-line transits.
NAMES.long: The safety factor $q$ is the ratio of toroidal to poloidal transits of a magnetic field line on a flux surface. It is the canonical measure of magnetic shear and stability in tokamak plasmas.

NAMES.name: plasma_current
NAMES.long: The total toroidal current flowing in the plasma column, $I_p$. Typically measured by flux loops and Rogowski coils and used as the primary control variable for ohmic and hybrid scenarios.
NAMES.sign: Positive in the direction of the toroidal magnetic field by convention.

NAMES.name: vacuum_toroidal_field
NAMES.short: Vacuum toroidal magnetic field $B_0$ at the reference radius $R_0$.
NAMES.long: The toroidal magnetic field that would be present at the device reference major radius $R_0$ in the absence of plasma, $B_0 = B_\phi(R_0)$. Used as the reference value for normalised parameters such as $\beta_N$.

NAMES.name: major_radius_of_magnetic_axis
NAMES.short: Major radius $R_{\mathrm{axis}}$ of the magnetic axis.
NAMES.long: The major radius coordinate $R$ of the magnetic axis $(\psi = 0)$. Combined with the vertical coordinate this fully locates the axis in the poloidal plane.
